nodes.title: Sustained funding for operations
nodes.text: CEEALAR requires approximately £12,500 ($15,500) monthly to operate, supporting an average of 20 residents at £625 ($750) per resident per month. Funding comes from individual donors, EA organizations, and grants.

connections.evidence: Direct operational dependency - without funding, accommodation and meals cannot be provided. Historical data shows consistent service delivery when funding is secure.
connections.assumptions: Funding continues to be available from EA community. Cost structure remains stable. No major operational disruptions.

connections.evidence: Funding enables staff time for application review, selection process, and community management essential for curated cohorts.
connections.assumptions: Selection process remains effective. Staff capacity is sufficient for quality curation.

nodes.title: Applications from EA-motivated individuals
nodes.text: CEEALAR receives applications from individuals seeking to transition into or advance within EA careers, particularly in AI safety, longtermist research, and related fields. Selection rate is typically 10-15%.

connections.evidence: Strong correlation between application quality and successful resident outcomes. Historical track record of residents transitioning to impactful roles at MIT, AI safety orgs, and starting initiatives like ML4Good UK.
connections.assumptions: EA community continues growing and producing quality candidates. CEEALAR reputation attracts genuinely motivated individuals.

nodes.title: Physical facility and operational systems
nodes.text: 17-room former hotel in Blackpool providing accommodation, workspaces, common areas, Wi-Fi, and basic operational infrastructure necessary for community living and focused work.

connections.evidence: Physical infrastructure directly enables accommodation provision. Facility has successfully housed residents since 2018.
connections.assumptions: Building remains in good condition. Local services and infrastructure continue to be available.

connections.evidence: Common spaces and work areas facilitate interaction. Infrastructure quality affects resident satisfaction and engagement levels.
connections.assumptions: Infrastructure design supports community building rather than hindering it.

nodes.title: Subsidized accommodation and meals
nodes.text: Core service providing residents with housing, meals, and basic living needs at significantly below-market rates, removing financial pressure and daily maintenance tasks.

connections.evidence: Consistent provision of accommodation and meals is fundamental to stable operations. Residents report high satisfaction with basic services when consistently delivered.
connections.assumptions: Service quality remains consistent. Cost structure stays manageable. No major operational disruptions.

nodes.title: Curated cohorts of EA-motivated residents
nodes.text: Carefully selected groups of residents chosen for their potential to make meaningful contributions to EA cause areas, creating a concentrated community of motivated individuals.

connections.evidence: Success stories from residents (MIT positions, ML4Good UK launch, AI safety org placements) build reputation over time. Quality curation maintains community standards.
connections.assumptions: Resident successes continue to be visible and attributed to CEEALAR. Selection criteria remain effective at identifying potential.

connections.evidence: Selection process has yielded residents who transition to impactful positions. 10-15% acceptance rate suggests rigorous screening.
connections.assumptions: Selection criteria accurately identify potential. High-potential individuals actually apply to program.

nodes.title: Access to EA networks and events
nodes.text: Connections to broader EA community including researchers, organizations, funding opportunities, and career pathways through CEEALAR's established relationships.

connections.evidence: Alumni frequently get jobs through CEEALAR connections. EA community values CEEALAR reputation and provides access to opportunities.
connections.assumptions: CEEALAR reputation remains strong in EA community. Networks continue to provide career value. EA job market remains healthy.

connections.evidence: Direct access to EA networks can provide immediate career opportunities, bypassing traditional skill development pathways for some residents.
connections.assumptions: Network connections translate to actual opportunities. Residents can leverage connections effectively.

nodes.title: Stable operations with adequate funding
nodes.text: CEEALAR maintains consistent service delivery with sufficient resources to house ~20 residents, provide meals, and support community activities without interruption.

connections.evidence: When operations are stable, residents report higher satisfaction and engagement. Operational consistency enables community building activities and reduces stress.
connections.assumptions: Operational stability translates to community confidence. Residents respond positively to consistent service delivery.

nodes.title: Strong reputation as valuable EA stepping stone
nodes.text: CEEALAR is recognized within EA community as effective program for career transitions, skill development, and producing impactful researchers and practitioners.

connections.evidence: Strong reputation attracts quality applicants and validates selection decisions. EA community endorsement supports resident confidence in program value.
connections.assumptions: Reputation continues to attract right candidates. Selection maintains quality standards that support reputation.

connections.evidence: Residents feel confident they're in a valuable program, supporting engagement. External validation from EA community reinforces commitment.
connections.assumptions: Reputation translates to resident confidence and engagement rather than complacency.

nodes.title: High-potential individuals are selected as residents
nodes.text: CEEALAR successfully identifies and attracts individuals with genuine potential for meaningful contributions to EA cause areas, particularly AI safety and longtermist research.

connections.evidence: High-potential residents are more likely to use focused time productively. Selection criteria include motivation and ability to engage in independent work.
connections.assumptions: Selected individuals actually have the potential identified. Potential translates to productive use of opportunities.

connections.evidence: High-potential individuals are more likely to build valuable professional relationships and be sought out by networks.
connections.assumptions: Potential is recognized by others in professional networks. Networks remain receptive to CEEALAR residents.

connections.evidence: High-potential residents are more likely to seek and benefit from mentorship opportunities available through CEEALAR connections.
connections.assumptions: Potential includes ability to receive and act on mentorship. Mentors remain available and engaged.

nodes.title: Residents integrate into supportive community
nodes.text: Residents successfully engage with house norms, participate in community activities, and create mutually supportive environment conducive to learning and development.

connections.evidence: Supportive community reduces stress and provides accountability for productive work. Peer support helps maintain focus and motivation.
connections.assumptions: Community support enhances rather than distracts from focused work. Residents use community accountability effectively.

connections.evidence: Living alongside other EAs naturally creates professional connections. Community activities facilitate introductions to broader EA networks.
connections.assumptions: Community relationships translate to lasting professional connections. Networks formed are professionally valuable.

connections.evidence: Supportive community environment makes residents more open to seeking and receiving mentorship. Peer mentorship also emerges naturally.
connections.assumptions: Community support includes effective mentorship culture. Residents actively seek and utilize guidance.

nodes.title: Deep, focused time for skill development and research
nodes.text: Without financial pressure and daily distractions, residents can concentrate full-time on learning machine learning, conducting research, or developing specialized expertise in their chosen EA cause areas.

connections.evidence: Multiple residents have successfully transitioned from other fields into AI safety research and ML through focused study. Removal of financial pressure enables full-time learning.
connections.assumptions: Residents actually use focused time productively rather than procrastinating. Learning efforts are directed toward relevant skills for EA careers.

nodes.title: Strong professional networks within EA community
nodes.text: Residents build lasting relationships with other EAs, researchers, and practitioners that provide career opportunities, collaboration prospects, and ongoing professional support.

connections.evidence: Professional networks frequently provide job referrals and career opportunities. Alumni often get positions through CEEALAR connections rather than traditional applications.
connections.assumptions: Networks remain valuable for career advancement over time. EA job market continues to value personal connections. Alumni maintain their positions and influence.

nodes.title: Mentorship and guidance on career transitions
nodes.text: Residents receive strategic advice on research directions, job applications, skill gaps, and career moves from experienced practitioners and researchers in EA community.

connections.evidence: Residents report valuable career guidance from CEEALAR networks. Mentorship helps focus skill development efforts and provides strategic career direction.
connections.assumptions: Mentorship is available from experienced EA practitioners. Residents actively seek and follow mentorship advice. Guidance translates to effective skill development.

nodes.title: Individuals develop skills, knowledge, and networks for EA careers
nodes.text: Promising individuals successfully acquire the technical skills, domain knowledge, and professional relationships necessary to make meaningful contributions to high-priority EA cause areas, particularly AI safety and longtermist research.

connections.evidence: More skilled researchers and practitioners working on AI alignment, biosecurity, and longtermist priorities directly contributes to risk reduction. Human capital is widely recognized as key bottleneck in EA cause areas.
connections.assumptions: Developed skills translate to actual contributions in EA cause areas. Career transitions result in net-positive impact rather than displacement of other potential contributors.

nodes.title: Reducing existential risks and improving long-term future
nodes.text: Contributing to humanity's ability to navigate critical risks including AI alignment, biosecurity threats, and other challenges that could determine the long-term trajectory of human civilization and flourishing.
